_comment: GreenGrid Neighborhood Simulation — ML Configuration Template

_instructions._1: This config extends the standard neighborhood config with ML solar generation.
_instructions._2: Set 'use_ml_solar': true to replace the synthetic math.sin() solar curve with predictions from the trained Sacramento linear regression model.
_instructions._3: When use_ml_solar is true, solar generation is driven by real Sacramento weather data (2006 NSRDB dataset) processed through the model in ML/solar_predictor.py.
_instructions._4: All other parameters (system sizing, grid, battery, strategy) work identically to the standard config.
_instructions._5: OVERRIDES: Use the overrides block to set unadvised sizing (fewer panels, 1 inverter, 1 battery) or well-designed sizing (scaled by household type and wealth level).
_instructions._6: Run via main.py > Mode 2 > Config 2 (ML Realistic).

simulation._duration_days_help: 360 = full year. Use 30 for quick tests.
simulation._time_step_minutes_help: 60 = hourly. ML predictor uses hourly (month, hour) lookups.
simulation._start_date_help: Simulation start date. Year does not affect ML predictions since the predictor uses (month, hour) averages from the 2006 Sacramento dataset.
simulation._season_help: Cloud coverage patterns for synthetic fallback only. Has no effect when use_ml_solar is true.
simulation._random_seed_help: Integer for reproducible results. null = different run each time.

_use_ml_solar_help: true = replace math.sin() solar generation with ML predictions from ML/solar_predictor.py. false = use standard synthetic sinusoidal model.

grid._import_cost_help: Cost to import energy in $/kWh (0.0075 = 0.75 cents).
grid._export_revenue_help: Revenue from exporting energy in $/kWh (0.009 = 0.9 cents).
grid._export_limit_help: Maximum power each household can export to grid in kW.

energy_management._strategy_help: Applied to all households. Options: LOAD_PRIORITY, CHARGE_PRIORITY, PRODUCE_PRIORITY.

inverter_defaults._comment: Applied to every inverter unit across all households unless overridden.

battery_defaults._comment: Applied to every battery unit across all households unless overridden.
battery_defaults._unit_capacity_help: Capacity per battery unit in kWh (13.5 = Tesla Powerwall).

solar_defaults._comment: Peak power per panel. Used by ML predictor: generation_kw = capacity_factor * n_panels * unit_peak_power_kw.

household_templates._comment: Default system sizing per household_type + wealth_level. These are the well-designed counts. For the unadvised scenario, override all houses in the overrides block.

neighborhood._mode_help: 'auto' for random distribution, 'manual' for exact counts.

manual._comment: Only active when mode is 'manual'. Generates 2 households per type+wealth combination (24 total).

overrides._comment: Use this block to set unadvised sizing (all 1s for studios, all 2s for families) or remove it entirely to use the well-designed household_templates above.
overrides._unadvised_example: Set solar_count=1, inverter_count=1, battery_count=1 for studios and solar_count=2, inverter_count=1, battery_count=1 for families to simulate a poorly designed installation.
overrides._well_designed_example: Remove overrides entirely or match the household_templates counts above.
